title: Unit 13 Test: Two-Dimensional Shapes and Partitioning

problems.question: How many sides does a hexagon have?

feedback.incorrect: Hexagon has 6 sides ('hex' = 6).

hints: 'Hex' means six.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Which is NOT a polygon?

feedback.incorrect: A circle is curved — not a polygon.

hints: Polygons have all straight sides.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: How many vertices does a pentagon have?

feedback.incorrect: Pentagon has 5 sides = 5 vertices.

hints: Vertices = sides for any polygon.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Which attribute is shared by ALL polygons?
choices: C) Closed with all straight sides

feedback.incorrect: All polygons are closed figures with all straight sides.

hints: What is true with no exceptions?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Which polygon ALWAYS has 4 right angles?

feedback.incorrect: A square always has 4 right angles.

hints: 4 right angles + 4 equal sides defines this shape.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: How many right angles does a right triangle have?

feedback.incorrect: A right triangle has exactly 1 right angle.

hints: The 'right' in the name tells you.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Which quadrilateral has 4 equal sides but no right angles (in general)?

feedback.correct: Right! 4 equal sides without right angles.
feedback.incorrect: Rhombus: 4 equal sides, no right angles (unless it's a square).

hints: Equal sides without 4 right angles.
problems.feedback_correct: Right! 4 equal sides without right angles.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Which describes a trapezoid?
choices: A) 4-sided polygon with at least 1 pair of parallel sides
C) A polygon with 4 right angles

feedback.incorrect: Trapezoid: 4 sides + at least 1 pair of parallel sides.

hints: Trapezoids have parallel sides — at least one pair.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: How many pairs of parallel sides does a parallelogram have?

feedback.incorrect: A parallelogram has 2 pairs of parallel sides.

hints: The name starts with 'parallel'.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: True or False: Every square is a rectangle.

feedback.incorrect: True. Squares fit the rectangle definition (4 sides + 4 right angles).

hints: Does a square fit the rectangle definition?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: What is the SAME for a square and a rectangle?

feedback.incorrect: Both have 4 right angles.

hints: Compare angles.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: What is DIFFERENT between a parallelogram and a trapezoid?
choices: A) Parallelogram has 2 pairs parallel; trapezoid has 1
B) Parallelogram has 4 sides; trapezoid has 5

feedback.incorrect: Difference: 2 pairs of parallel sides vs 1 pair.

hints: Compare parallel pairs.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: A circle is divided into 4 equal parts. Each part is what fraction? (Write as 1/n)

feedback.incorrect: Each of 4 equal parts is 1/4.

hints: 1 of 4 equal parts.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: A square is divided into 8 equal parts. Each part is a __

feedback.incorrect: 8 equal parts → eighths.

hints: 8 equal parts has a special name.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Which is BIGGER on the same whole: 1/3 or 1/8?

feedback.incorrect: 1/3 is bigger. Fewer parts = bigger pieces.

hints: Smaller denominator = bigger piece.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: A rectangle has area 24 sq cm and is divided into 6 equal parts. Find the area of each part. (Write the number.)

feedback.incorrect: Each part = 24 ÷ 6 = 4 sq cm.

hints: Divide whole area by number of parts.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Which is a unit fraction?

feedback.incorrect: Unit fractions have 1 as numerator.

hints: Top number must be 1.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: If you put 5 pieces of 1/8 together, what fraction do you get? (Write as a fraction)
hints: Add tops; keep bottom.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: Anna's quadrilateral has 4 right angles AND 4 equal sides. What is it?

feedback.incorrect: 4 right angles AND 4 equal sides = square.

hints: Both attributes together.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

problems.question: A rectangle has area 36 sq cm in 4 equal parts. What is the TOTAL area of 3 of these parts? (Write the number.)

feedback.incorrect: Each part = 36 ÷ 4 = 9. 3 parts = 3 × 9 = 27 sq cm.

hints: Find one part first, then multiply.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/unit_test
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

pretest.question: Which polygon has 0 right angles?
choices: D) A figure shaped like the letter L

D.note: An L-shape has right angles at its corners

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L1_describe_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Which choice has no square corners?

feedback.correct: Yes! A regular pentagon has 5 angles, but none of them are right angles.
feedback.incorrect: A regular pentagon has 0 right angles. Squares, rectangles, and L-shapes all have right angles.

pretest.feedback_correct: Yes! A regular pentagon has 5 angles, but none of them are right angles.

pretest.question: On the same whole, which is the SMALLEST piece?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L5_partition_shapes
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Which has the most equal parts?

feedback.correct: Right! 1/8 has the most equal parts, so each piece is the smallest.
feedback.incorrect: 1/8 is the smallest. More parts = smaller pieces.

pretest.feedback_correct: Right! 1/8 has the most equal parts, so each piece is the smallest.

pretest.question: A rectangle is divided into 6 equal parts. Each part is a __

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L5_partition_shapes
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: 6 parts has a name ending in 'th'.

feedback.incorrect: 6 equal parts are called sixths. Each is 1/6.

pretest.question: Which is a unit fraction?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L6_unit_fractions_area
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Top number must be 1.

feedback.correct: Right! 1/8 has 1 as the numerator — it is a unit fraction.
feedback.incorrect: Unit fractions need 1 on top. 1/8 fits.

pretest.feedback_correct: Right! 1/8 has 1 as the numerator — it is a unit fraction.

pretest.question: Lily says, 'My polygon has more sides than a pentagon but fewer sides than an octagon.' Which polygon could Lily have drawn? (Choose any one that fits.)
solution_steps: Step 1: Pentagon has 5 sides; octagon has 8 sides.
Step 2: Lily's polygon has more than 5 and fewer than 8 — so 6 or 7.
Step 3: Among the choices, only hexagon (6 sides) fits.

feedback.correct: Right! Hexagon has 6 sides, which is between 5 and 8.
feedback.incorrect: Lily's polygon has between 5 and 8 sides. Only the hexagon (6 sides) fits.

pretest.three_read_prompt: Read 1: How many sides do pentagon and octagon have? Read 2: What range fits 'more than 5 but fewer than 8'? Read 3: Which choice has that many sides?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L1_describe_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: How many sides do pentagon and octagon have? What numbers fit between them?
pretest.feedback_correct: Right! Hexagon has 6 sides, which is between 5 and 8.

pretest.question: A rectangle is partitioned into 4 equal parts. The area of EACH part is 7 sq cm. What is the area of the WHOLE rectangle?

feedback.incorrect: Whole = parts × part area = 4 × 7 = 28 sq cm.

pretest.three_read_prompt: Read 1: How many parts? Read 2: How big is each part? Read 3: How big is the whole?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L6_unit_fractions_area
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Multiply parts by area of each.

pretest.question: True or False: All triangles have at least one right angle.

true.note: Only RIGHT triangles have a right angle. Equilateral triangles have none.

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Think about an equilateral triangle. Does it have a right angle?

feedback.correct: False! Only right triangles have a right angle. Equilateral triangles have none.
feedback.incorrect: False. Some triangles have no right angles — for example, equilateral triangles.

pretest.feedback_correct: False! Only right triangles have a right angle. Equilateral triangles have none.

pretest.question: Which group of polygons all share the attribute 'all sides equal'?

B.note: Scalene triangle has all DIFFERENT sides

C.note: Rectangle and scalene triangle do not have all equal sides

D.note: Rectangle and trapezoid do not have all equal sides

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L2_classify_polygons
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Square, equilateral triangle, rhombus: all equal sides.

hints: Which group has every shape with all sides matching?

feedback.correct: Right! Squares (4=), equilateral triangles (3=), and rhombuses (4=) all have equal sides.
feedback.incorrect: All members must have all equal sides. Only choice A — square, equilateral triangle, rhombus — qualifies.

pretest.feedback_correct: Right! Squares (4=), equilateral triangles (3=), and rhombuses (4=) all have equal sides.

pretest.question: Which quadrilateral has 4 right angles AND 2 long sides + 2 short sides?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Rectangle (not square): 2 pairs of equal opposite sides.

hints: 4 right angles, sides not all equal — what is it?

feedback.incorrect: Non-square rectangle: 4 right angles + 2 long & 2 short sides.

pretest.question: Lily makes a chart comparing 4 shapes: square, rectangle, rhombus, parallelogram. She lists 'has 4 right angles.' Which TWO shapes get a YES?

A.note: Parallelogram does not always have right angles

B.note: Non-square rhombus has no right angles

D.note: Only square and rectangle always have right angles

feedback.correct: Right! Square and rectangle always have 4 right angles. The other two do not.
feedback.incorrect: Square and rectangle always have 4 right angles. Rhombus and parallelogram do not (unless they ARE rectangles).

pretest.three_read_prompt: Read 1: Which 4 shapes is Lily checking? Read 2: Which always have 4 right angles? Read 3: Which two shapes match?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L4_compare_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Test each shape against the rule '4 right angles always'.
pretest.feedback_correct: Right! Square and rectangle always have 4 right angles. The other two do not.

pretest.question: True or False: Every quadrilateral is a parallelogram.

true.note: Some quadrilaterals (like trapezoids) are NOT parallelograms

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L3_identify_quadrilaterals
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015
verification.original_ref: Trapezoids are quadrilaterals but not parallelograms.

hints: Are trapezoids parallelograms?

feedback.correct: False! Some quadrilaterals — like trapezoids with only 1 pair of parallel sides — are NOT parallelograms.
feedback.incorrect: False. Not every quadrilateral is a parallelogram. A trapezoid with only 1 pair of parallel sides is not.

pretest.feedback_correct: False! Some quadrilaterals — like trapezoids with only 1 pair of parallel sides — are NOT parallelograms.

pretest.question: Anna's pizza has 4 equal slices. Lily's same-size pizza has 8 equal slices. Whose slice is BIGGER?

verification.concept_source: Go Math Grade 3 Ch.13 Lesson — U13_shapes/L7_mixed_shapes_fractions
verification.assessment_source: Smarter Balanced Grade 3 Mathematics Item Specifications 2015

hints: Anna has fewer slices.

feedback.correct: Right! Anna's slice (1/4) is bigger than Lily's (1/8).
feedback.incorrect: Anna's slice is bigger. Fewer slices = bigger pieces.

pretest.feedback_correct: Right! Anna's slice (1/4) is bigger than Lily's (1/8).
